meta.name: anubis-production
meta.contract: Fields aqi, retrieval, latency, security are read by bin/experiment.js. Do not rename or move them. New fields (baseline, priorities, keepRevertRules, stopCondition, loop) are read by the codex orchestrator.

priorities.rationale: One query (invoice approval, VID-APPROVAL-005) returns zero true positives. Until this is fixed, AQI cannot pass the 80 hard cap and production is unreachable.

Largest quality gap not yet at production. Score breakdowns show bottom-half of top-10 is graph-only or entity-only hits with weak BM25 / vector signal — candidates for downranking, pruning, or rerank.
Composite. Moves as #1 and #2 land; do not optimise directly.
Close — protect, do not regress. Final push only after precision is at target.
Evidence is computed on read; baseline coverage 4%. Needs evidence persisted on write or a coverage walker before this can move.

priorities.blockedBy: harness does not yet split candidate vs visible edges
priorities.rationale: Baseline has 71 edges/chunk and no visibility split. Requires a trusted-graph / candidate-graph separation in both engine and benchmark before this is observable.

revertIfAny.reason: Recall is the strongest baseline signal — protect it (program.md rule 3).
More edges without precision gain is added noise.

keepIfAny.reason: Composite improvement worth keeping (matches bin/experiment.js decision logic).

stopCondition.description: Engine is production-ready when every productionChecks entry from bin/experiment.js passes against the quick suite AND AQI clears 95 with a 90 hard minimum and without a hard-gate cap.

loop.description: One iteration of the self-improving benchmark loop. Codex executes this against the current baseline until stopCondition is true.
iteration: Read baseline.snapshot and priorities[]. Pick the highest-rank priority whose target is not met and is not blockedBy harness work.
Form ONE hypothesis that targets that metric. Verify the hypothesis cannot trigger any revertIfAny rule by construction.
Scaffold experiments/NNNN-<slug>/ with config.json (suite=quick, baseline=current), experiment.md (hypothesis + change scope), and a single code change.
Apply keepRevertRules to result.json. Persist decision.md and result.json.
If decision=keep: write new baselines/<name>.json, update meta.updatedAt and baseline.{ref,lastExperiment,snapshot} in this file, recompute priorities[].{current,gap}.
If decision=revert: leave baseline untouched, mark the hypothesis as tried in the experiment's decision.md, pick the next hypothesis.
Re-evaluate stopCondition. If all true, halt and report production-ready.
constraints: One meaningful change per experiment (program.md rule 1).
Never relax benchmark expectations to pass (rule 2).
Every result.json must include score breakdown (rule 7).
Every graph edge must have evidence or be hidden from the trusted graph (rule 8).

suite.fullPromotion: Re-run against retrieval + graph + security suites before promoting to baseline when a change touches retrieval scoring, graph construction, or auth/permissions.
